data.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I receive automatic friction signals from session tool logs
data.description:
## Description

**What**: A stop hook that scans tool-error-counter.json and tool-use-*.jsonl at session end and records a mechanical-friction suggestion when configurable thresholds are exceeded
**Why**: Automatic capture catches friction the agent does not self-identify — retry storms, repeated errors, timeouts. These quantitative signals complement the qualitative agent-driven path
**Scope**:
- IN: Stop hook implementation, configurable thresholds in dso-config.conf (suggestion.error_threshold, suggestion.timeout_threshold), conservative defaults derived from development environment data, calls suggestion-record.sh with source=stop-hook
- OUT: Agent-driven capture (Story 2), sub-agent sentinel (Story 4), retro integration (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a stop hook scans tool-error-counter.json and tool-use-*.jsonl at session end and records a mechanical-friction suggestion when thresholds are exceeded
  ← Satisfies: "A stop hook scans tool-error-counter.json and tool-use-*.jsonl at session end and records a mechanical-friction suggestion"
- When this story is complete, thresholds are configurable via suggestion.error_threshold and suggestion.timeout_threshold in dso-config.conf
  ← Satisfies: "configurable thresholds are exceeded (suggestion.error_threshold and suggestion.timeout_threshold in dso-config.conf)"
- When this story is complete, default thresholds are conservative values derived from inspecting actual tool-use logs in the development environment
  ← Satisfies: "with conservative defaults derived from development environment data"
- When this story is complete, the hook gracefully handles missing or malformed tool-error-counter.json without crashing
  ← Satisfies: scenario analysis finding "tool-error-counter.json missing causes stop hook crash"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must guard against missing tool-error-counter.json (fresh environment, no-error sessions) — fail silently, never crash the hook
- [Performance] Large tool-use-*.jsonl files need bounded reads (tail last N lines or streaming count) to stay within hook timeout ceiling (~73s)
- [Reliability] Hook must be registered in the stop hook dispatcher — verify existing install path handles upgrade
